claim: Start every project with a PRD (Product Requirement Document) in markdown that defines the entire scope of work and serves as the north star for coding agents
actionable: Create a markdown PRD with target users, mission, in-scope/out-of-scope items, and architecture before breaking into features; reference it in every agent conversation.
source_title: The 5 Techniques Separating Top Agentic Engineers Right Now

claim: Keep your global rules file lightweight (under 200 lines) and reference task-specific context in separate markdown files loaded only when needed
actionable: Move detailed instructions into modular reference files in a .claude/reference folder; reference them in global rules only for specific task types.
source_title: The 5 Techniques Separating Top Agentic Engineers Right Now

claim: Turn any prompt you send to a coding agent more than twice into a reusable command or workflow documented in markdown
actionable: Create /commands folder with .md files for recurring workflows (e.g., /prime to load codebase context, /plan to structure features, /execute to build).
source_title: The 5 Techniques Separating Top Agentic Engineers Right Now

claim: Reset your context between planning and execution: plan in one conversation thread, output a structured plan document, then start fresh with just that plan
actionable: After planning, clear the context window and restart; feed only the structured plan into execution to preserve context for actual coding and reasoning.
source_title: The 5 Techniques Separating Top Agentic Engineers Right Now

claim: Treat every bug as an opportunity to improve your system: update global rules, reference docs, or commands to prevent the issue from happening again
actionable: After fixing a bug, ask the agent: what in our rules, commands, or process should we change so this doesn't happen again? Update your system based on its answer.
source_title: The 5 Techniques Separating Top Agentic Engineers Right Now

claim: Use structured plans as execution context that include feature description, user story, task breakdown, and architecture references but no other loaded context
actionable: Create structured plan documents (e.g., /plan output) that are comprehensive enough to stand alone; use them as the sole context input for the execution phase.
source_title: The 5 Techniques Separating Top Agentic Engineers Right Now
